title: How I Feel
description: How do you feel when Nani comes to visit?  How do you feel when you spoil a library book? Read this book and see how you feel today.

pages.text:
Sometimes my heart beats fast.
Sometimes I scream and shout.
Some things make me giggle.
Other things make me pout.

Can you tell me how I feel?
And can you tell me why?
Can you tell me why I smile?
What makes me want to cry?

Sssss! Do you hear that?
What is that hissing sound?
I freeze, I gasp, I tremble.
My eyes grow big and round.
Can you tell me how I feel?

Wheeeee! Today is special.
Nani is coming to stay!
My face glows, my eyes shine.
I jump and shout, “Hurray!”
Can you tell me how I feel?

Ewwwww!
My shoes are stinky.
I think I stepped in poop.
I clench my fists. I wrinkle my nose.
Yuck! Yucky doo!
Can you tell me how I feel?

I have to return this library book,
but I tore it by mistake.
I chew my nails. I sweat a bit.
My knees begin to shake.
Can you tell me how I feel?

Is that enormous package really a gift—for me?
My mouth falls open. My eyebrows fly up.
I wonder what it could be.
Can you tell me how I feel?

Iiish! I squirm and shuffle my feet.
I told a lie today.
I hang my head. My face is red.
I don’t know what to say.
Can you tell me how I feel?

Sniff! Nani is leaving.
I stand and wave goodbye.
My mouth curves down,
my eyes are red, and I begin to cry.
Can you tell me how I feel?

Grrrr! I’m not sleepy!
I don’t want to go to bed!
I gnash my teeth. I stomp my feet.
My face is fiery red.
Can you tell me how I feel?

Mamma’s hugs are lovely.
Cuddly, warm and tight.
I sigh and smile
and kiss her cheek.
My eyes are big and bright.
Can you tell me how I feel?
